components.name: Badge
components.description: A small, saturated indicator that emphasises a count, status or label.
whenToUse: Counters on navigation items (8 unread)
Status pills inside tables or cards
Featured / new / beta indicators
Numeric or short status labels that demand emphasis
whenNotToUse: For removable filter chips — use Tag
For long labels — use Tag with a softer palette
For navigation — use Link

components.name: Button
components.description: Button Button es un elemento interactivo que desencadena acciones o navega al usuario dentro de la interfaz. Disponible en múltiples variantes, tamaños y estados para adaptarse a distintos niveles de jerarquía visual y contextos de uso. Vista previa Cuándo usarlo Confirmar o enviar un formulario como acción principal de la pantalla. Desencadenar la apertura de un diálogo, modal o panel lateral. Ofrecer una acción alternativa o secundaria junto a la acción primaria. Ejecutar acciones destructivas que requieren confirmación explícita del usuario. Representar acciones compactas mediante iconos cuando el espacio es limitado y la acción es reconocible sin texto. Cuándo NO usarlo No uses Button para navegar a una URL externa cuando un enlace de texto en línea es semánticamente más apropiado. No uses múltiples botones de variante default en la misma sección; reserva el énfasis máximo para una única acción primaria. No uses un botón icon-only sin aria-label cuando la acción no es universalmente reconocible por su icono. No uses Button para representar estados informativos o de selección persistente; considera Toggle o Checkbox en su lugar. Anatomía Variantes El componente expone seis variantes de estilo (default, secondary, destructive, outline, ghost, link) y ocho tamaños que incluyen versiones estándar e icon-only, permitiendo cubrir desde acciones primarias destacadas hasta controles compactos en toolbars. Variant Tamaños Estados Accesibilidad Mantén un anillo de foco visible y de alto contraste en el estado focus para usuarios de teclado y switch access. Los botones icon-only deben incluir aria-label descriptivo que comunique la acción, ya que el icono no es leído por lectores de pantalla. Los botones en estado disabled deben tener el atributo disabled o aria-disabled='true' para que los lectores de pantalla no los anuncien como accionables. El elemento raíz debe ser <button> o role='button' para garantizar la activación con Enter y Spacebar por defecto. Los botones destructivos deben comunicar la naturaleza irreversible de la acción; considera añadir un mensaje de confirmación accesible antes de ejecutarla. Asegura una relación de contraste mínima de 4.5:1 entre el texto del label y el fondo del botón en todos los estados visibles. Do / Don't Referencias
whenToUse: Confirming or submitting a form
Triggering a primary action on a screen
Launching a dialog, drawer or destructive flow
Grouping a decision pair (Cancel / Confirm)
whenNotToUse: For in-page navigation to another URL — use Link
For toggling state on/off — use Switch or a selectable control
When the visual is just decoration with no action

components.name: ButtonGroup
components.description: A horizontally or vertically aligned set of related Buttons, typically the commit pair of a form or dialog.
whenToUse: The footer of a dialog, drawer or form
A confirm / cancel pair at the end of a flow
A small set of mutually relevant CTAs
When you need consistent spacing between commit actions
whenNotToUse: For segmented selection — use ActionGroup
For dense toolbars — use ActionBar
When buttons are unrelated and live across the page

components.name: Card
components.description: A self-contained surface that groups a heading, body, optional media and actions about a single entity.
whenToUse: Items in a feed, gallery or grid
Summarising an entity with a CTA (product, project, article)
Dashboard tiles that pair text, image and actions
Catalogues with mixed text and media
whenNotToUse: For dense rows — use a Table
For static text blocks — render plain content
For interactive controls only — use Button or ActionGroup

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: A toggle that selects or deselects an independent option; multiple checkboxes can be checked at once.
whenToUse: Selecting any number of options in a list
Opting in or out of a single preference
Picking multiple filters or facets
Bulk-select rows in a table
whenNotToUse: For one-of-many selection — use Radio
For a binary on/off toggle that takes effect immediately — use Switch
When the option fundamentally changes the surface — use a different control

components.name: Field
components.description: shadcn/ui Field. Label + ONE control slot + description + error. The control is a real nested instance of any saved component (Input by default) — pick it in the panel, swap it natively in Figma. The choiceCard option wraps the slot in a bordered, checkable container. `responsive` orientation renders as vertical in the IR (no container queries in Figma). Fully bound to shadcn semantic tokens.

components.name: Input
components.description: A single-line text field for free-form entry such as names, emails or short values.
whenToUse: Collecting a single line of text or a short value
Email, name, URL, search-like queries (when SearchField doesn't fit)
Inline editing of a discrete property
Inside a Form alongside other controls
whenNotToUse: For numeric input with stepping — use NumberField
For multi-line content — use TextArea
When the value belongs to a finite list — use Select / ComboBox

components.name: FieldGroup
components.description: shadcn/ui FieldGroup. A gap-7 column of composed Fields (real instances of your saved components, grouped by the body composer) with an optional 'Or' FieldSeparator between groups. Fully bound to shadcn semantic tokens.

components.name: Input
components.description: A single-line text field for free-form entry such as names, emails or short values.
whenToUse: Collecting a single line of text or a short value
Email, name, URL, search-like queries (when SearchField doesn't fit)
Inline editing of a discrete property
Inside a Form alongside other controls
whenNotToUse: For numeric input with stepping — use NumberField
For multi-line content — use TextArea
When the value belongs to a finite list — use Select / ComboBox

components.name: InputGroup
components.description: shadcn/ui InputGroup. A single bordered row composing a borderless input with leading/trailing addons (text, icon, or a small button) sharing the group's border. States: default, focus (group ring), disabled. Fully bound to shadcn semantic tokens.

components.name: Label
components.description: shadcn/ui Label. A single inline form label (text-sm medium) bound to shadcn semantic + typography tokens. Disabled state dims to 50% opacity.

components.name: RadioGroup
components.description: A labelled set of Radios where exactly one option must be selected.
whenToUse: Single-choice questions in forms
Configurator steps with 2–7 fixed alternatives
Toggling between presets where the trade-off is visible
Setting selection with a sensible default
whenNotToUse: For multi-select — use CheckboxGroup
For more than ~7 options — use Select
For binary toggles that take effect immediately — use Switch

components.name: Select
components.description: A control that opens a list of options and displays the chosen one in a trigger field.
whenToUse: Picking one value from a known finite list
Forms with 4–20 options where space matters
Country, language, role or status pickers
When typing is unnecessary
whenNotToUse: For free-form text or autocomplete — use ComboBox
For 2–4 visible options — use RadioGroup
For mutually-exclusive nav — use Tabs

components.name: Switch
components.description: A binary control that turns a setting on or off and applies the change immediately.
whenToUse: Toggling a system or feature setting (Wi-Fi, notifications)
Switching between two states without confirmation
An always-visible preference that affects the surface
Anywhere users expect an OS-like toggle
whenNotToUse: For form submission style choices — use Checkbox
For one-of-many selection — use Radio
When the change requires explicit confirmation — use Button

components.name: Textarea
components.description: shadcn/ui Textarea. Multi-line text field across sizes (sm, md, lg) — rounded-md, `input` border, body-sm placeholder in muted-foreground, focus ring on `ring`, destructive border + halo on error (aria-invalid), disabled dims to 50%. Fully bound to shadcn semantic tokens.

components.name: ToggleGroup
components.description: shadcn/ui ToggleGroup. A row of toggle items sharing the Toggle styling — joined (spacing 0, shared border, end-only radius) or gapped. type single / multiple. The pressed set bakes from a comma index list; the preview is a genuine Radix ToggleGroup. Fully bound to shadcn semantic tokens.
